key_points: India is located in the continent of Asia
India lies entirely in the Northern Hemisphere
The mainland of India extends between latitudes 8°4'N and 37°6'N
The Tropic of Cancer (23°30'N) passes almost through the middle of India
The Tropic of Cancer divides India into tropical and subtropical regions
India is the seventh largest country in the world by area
India has a total geographical area of about 3.28 million square kilometres
India is surrounded by the Arabian Sea in the west, the Bay of Bengal in the east and the Indian Ocean in the south
India's central location helped it establish trade and cultural contacts with other regions
examples: Example of sea surrounding India: Arabian Sea in the west
Example of sea surrounding India: Bay of Bengal in the east
Example of ocean surrounding India: Indian Ocean in the south
Example of island group: Lakshadweep Islands in the Arabian Sea
Example of island group: Andaman and Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal
Example of island neighbour: Sri Lanka
formulas: Area of India ≈ 3.28 million square kilometres
exam_tips: Remember the latitudinal and longitudinal extent of India for map-based questions
Understand the importance of the Tropic of Cancer in dividing India's climatic regions
Focus on India's central location and its impact on trade and cultural contacts
Learn the neighbouring countries and island groups of India
Practice locating seas, oceans and neighbouring countries on the map
